full_prompt: Design a topology-optimized monolithic bracket for an open-source industrial-FDM bracket redesign benchmark, printable on a large-format FDM system in ULTEM 9085 (yield 69 MPa, E 2.51 GPa, density 1340 kg/m^3), replacing a traditionally machined aluminum cable-tray support bracket. The build envelope is 406 mm x 355 mm x 406 mm (16 x 14 x 16 inches). The bracket must fit a 200 mm x 150 mm x 100 mm design space envelope and mount to an aircraft interior floor beam via four 10 mm bolt holes on a 150 x 80 mm rectangular pattern, supporting a cable tray load of 700 N vertical plus 300 N lateral at a mounting stud 150 mm offset from the floor plane. The bracket must simultaneously satisfy: (R1) max von Mises stress under vertical 700 N plus lateral 300 N combined case must not exceed 46 MPa (ULTEM 9085 yield 69 / SF 1.5), (R2) max displacement at the stud must not exceed 1.5 mm under combined load, (R3) mass must be at least 40% lower than a baseline solid-aluminum bracket filling the design envelope (baseline 6061 block mass = 200*150*100*2700/1e9 = 8.1 kg), so the redesigned ULTEM bracket must be under 4.86 kg (with ULTEM density 1340 vs Al 2700 this is essentially automatic, but the rule is the 40% reduction vs bulk-block), (R4) must print without support in the FDM build volume (self-supporting down to 45 degrees from build plate), and (R5) a linear buckling analysis under 2x combined load must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver an STL ready for any FDM slicer plus STEP AP242 and a verification report with max von Mises, stud displacement, bracket mass, print orientation, and first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Topology-optimize a monolithic cable-tray support bracket, printable on a large-format FDM system in ULTEM 9085, replacing a traditionally machined aluminum baseline. Design space is 200 x 150 x 100 mm within a 406 x 355 x 406 mm FDM build envelope. The bracket mounts to an aircraft-interior floor beam via four 10 mm bolts on a 150 x 80 mm pattern and carries a cable-tray stud 150 mm offset from the floor plane. Bolt holes are fully fixed (6 DOF).

Load cases.
- LC1 vertical + lateral: 700 N vertical plus 300 N lateral simultaneously at the stud.
- LC2 buckling: 2x combined (1400 N vertical + 600 N lateral).

Bracket mass target is at least 40% lighter than a solid-aluminum-block baseline filling the design space. The part must be self-supporting on FDM at 45 degrees from the build plate.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 40% mass reduction with self-supporting constraint is typically delivered by overhang-aware topology or lattice optimization around the structural FEA.
examples: Altair OptiStruct with AM overhang filter

prompt.title: Large-format FDM ULTEM 9085 cable-tray bracket
prompt.brief: Redesign a cable-tray support bracket in ULTEM 9085, printable on a large-format FDM platform (406 x 355 x 406 mm), carrying 700 N + 300 N with 40% mass reduction vs bulk-block and 2x buckling margin.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Not part of the submission rubric.
